mentions.note: Gave up post-earnings gains similar to 1999; no reason to believe rebound coming in current environment.

CEO Ramon Laguarta winning through innovation (small pack sizes for GLP-1 users, Gatorade refresh, Poppi), selective pricing, global sourcing, scale advantage. Up $3+ on better-than-expected quarter. CPG despair = opportunity for disciplined operator.
Laguarta's innovation (small-form packs, Gatorade refresh), cost control (aluminum hedges, global sourcing), and execution beating headwinds; strong Q1 guidance.
Second-best packaged food company (after Coca-Cola); CEO Raml Magguire navigating GLP-1 headwinds and healthy diet trends well; listening to customers.
One of February's winning prosaic brands with popular appeal; defensive consumer staple that outperformed tech/hardware.
Consumer staple benefiting from rotation to 'understandable' companies making products people want; tangible demand, pricing power in inflationary environment.
Stock went insane this week; growth in snacks but from price cuts not volume; signals weak economy; cheap defensive recession-proof name.
Beverage/snack company rallying despite GLP-1 headwind fears; diversified earnings and dividend support; benefiting from AI productivity narrative.
Surged from 144 to 155 in seven sessions; reports tomorrow; uncertain if still a buy after 7.6% move.
Worry about snack division struggling from GLP-1 weight loss drug adoption; CEO Magguire could take serious actions to lessen snacks dependence; stock closed up 5 points today.
CPG stuck in mud; 4%+ yield but no upside due to RFK Jr. junk-food opposition and GLP-1 weight-loss drugs killing demand; only hope is breakup strategy like Kellogg model.
